corpus_policy.source: Task shapes may use runtime/host-orchestrator/fixtures/wave1-smokes only as historical shape references. Their fake-worker outputs and Hermes-era metadata are not comparative results, live Codex acceptance, or success-oracle evidence for this evaluation.
corpus_policy.execution_workspace: Every write trial starts from a newly created disposable detached worktree at the declared commit. The primary worktree is never a trial workspace.
corpus_policy.task_input_mutability: Task inputs, oracle text, eligible variants, repeat count, and counterbalancing order are sealed before the first trial.

task_input.prompt: In a detached worktree at the declared snapshot, report the exact HEAD and tree, identify the current baseline candidate ID from planning-status.json, identify the current selected work item, and state whether runtime/local-ai-runtime exists. Do not edit files, change configuration, or contact a remote service.

required_facts: the report distinguishes the historical snapshot state from the v3.23 evaluation contract state
success_oracle.write_rule: git diff --exit-code must succeed before cleanup.

task_input.prompt: Add one focused test under runtime/host-orchestrator/tests/test_cli_entrypoint.py that constructs the existing CLI parser and proves the existing --print-layout and --run-wave1-smokes flags are discoverable. Do not change production code, runtime configuration, or any other test file.

required_facts: exactly one declared test file is modified
the new test exercises build_parser rather than a hand-written flag list
the test asserts both existing flags
success_oracle.write_rule: The disposable worktree is either clean after rollback or has a recorded patch digest before disposal.

recovery_and_rollback.success_condition: git restore --source=HEAD -- runtime/host-orchestrator/tests/test_cli_entrypoint.py followed by git diff --exit-code succeeds in the disposable worktree.

task_input.prompt: Add one focused test in runtime/host-orchestrator/tests/test_planning_governance.py that invokes the planning verifier JSON-object loader with a literal duplicate-key object and asserts that duplicate keys are rejected. Do not alter verifier production code or planning-state files.

required_facts: the test uses a literal JSON duplicate key rather than a Python mapping
the test asserts the duplicate-key error path
no planning control-plane file or verifier production source is modified
success_oracle.write_rule: The disposable worktree is either clean after rollback or has a recorded patch digest before disposal.

recovery_and_rollback.success_condition: git restore --source=HEAD -- runtime/host-orchestrator/tests/test_planning_governance.py followed by git diff --exit-code succeeds in the disposable worktree.

counterbalancing.rule: For every task family and repeat index, rotate the sealed eligible-variant order by counterbalance block. Record skipped conditional variants as predeclared not_applicable with the reason; do not replace them after observing a result.
counterbalancing.carryover_rule: No worktree, conversation, patch, tool session, or recovery result may carry from one variant or repeat into another.

denominator_rules.core_comparison: Every declared applicable thin_codex_native and native_plus_key_gates trial is in the denominator, including failures, stops, conflicts, rework, recovery attempts, and inconclusive outcomes.
denominator_rules.conditional_harness_comparison: Report eligible, attempted, not_applicable, and excluded-by-stop counts separately for each conditional harness. Predeclared not_applicable is not a failure and is not silently counted as a successful replacement claim.
denominator_rules.downstream_outcome: Every sampled downstream observation remains in the denominator. censored and unknown are distinct visible values and are not passes.
denominator_rules.recovery_rollback: Every declared recovery or rollback attempt is in the recovery denominator; inability to reproduce a required rollback stops the evaluation.
